name: Tigre

sources.author: Raz, Shlomo
sources.title: Tigre Grammar and Texts
sources.notes: Reference grammar; the source for a future syllabary map.

notes: Tigre (ትግሬ, also Tigrayit/Xasa) — North Ethiopic Semitic language of Eritrea and eastern Sudan, written in the Geʽez (Ethiopic) script. INPUT CONTRACT: Geʽez is an ABUGIDA — each glyph is a consonant + one of seven vowel orders — so a grapheme map must enumerate the syllabograms, not letters; THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED here (graphemes/allophones are empty). Tigre also uses the script with a different sound value set from Amharic/Tigrinya (it retains the pharyngeals and lacks ejective/other mergers), so it cannot inherit an existing Ethiopic map. Wikidata gives no ISO 639-3 or Glottolog code for this Wikimedia item (the language is tig / tigr1247 in those registries, but that value is not asserted here since it was not verified from Wikidata).

ancestors.notes: Semitic — the classification node this language sits under.
